panels.title: Halted nodes
panels.description:
Nodes whose consensus halt-gate is tripped (stale rounds / sub-quorum / fork).

Normal: 0.
Act if: >0 sustained, a node has stopped finalizing.

panels.title: Divergence detections (15m, auto-healed)
panels.description:
**Divergence detections (15m)** - Rate of state-root divergences anti-entropy *detected and auto-healed* in the last 15m (`tip_consensus_divergence_total` is a lifetime counter; this is its windowed increase).

**Healthy**: 0 at steady state. Non-zero during catch-up/rejoin is EXPECTED and benign - a behind node legitimately differs from the frontier until it finishes resyncing; the canary fires, then the resync reconciles it. Yellow = recent reconcile activity to be aware of, not an emergency.

**The real fork emergency** is the 'Byzantine fork (halted / detection round)' panel: that fires only when a node could NOT reconcile and halted. Watch that one for paging.

panels.title: Nodes online
panels.description:
Nodes whose /metrics responded to the last scrape.

Normal: 5.
Act if: <4 (below quorum), a node's thread is blocked or it crashed.

panels.title: Quorum margin (min)
panels.description:
Smallest (active participants minus quorum) across the federation.

Normal: >=1.
Act if: 0, the federation is at the edge; any single drop halts it.

panels.title: Quorum threshold
panels.description:
**Quorum threshold** - The 2f+1 majority needed for Bullshark to commit an anchor, derived from the active committee size. For N nodes: quorum = floor(2N/3) + 1.

**Healthy**: stable value matching the active committee.

**Trouble**: changes unexpectedly = committee membership shifted (could be legitimate auto-derivation from `recent_producers` or a node being silently dropped).

panels.title: Worst event-loop stall
panels.description:
Longest single event-loop block in the federation (last 1s window).

Normal: <250ms.
Act if: >1000ms sustained, the thread is starved; that node drops peers and goes offline.

panels.title: Suspect peers
panels.description:
Peers any node currently can't reach via heartbeat.

Normal: 0.
Act if: >0 sustained, each suspect triggers an anti-entropy sync storm (the jitter feedback loop).

panels.title: Commit rate
panels.description:
Anchors committed per minute across the federation.

Normal: >0 (advancing).
Act if: 0, consensus is not finalizing anything.

panels.title: Committed round per node
panels.description:
The last round each node finalized.

Normal: all lines rise together, tightly bunched.
Act if: a line flatlines (that node stalled) or sits far below the others (sync gap).

panels.title: Commit spread: leader minus laggard
panels.description:
How many rounds the most-behind node trails the leader.

Normal: <5.
Act if: >20 sustained, a node is falling behind and anti-entropy isn't closing the gap.

panels.title: Consensus index (anchor commits since genesis) - per node
panels.description:
**Consensus index** - Network-wide count of anchor commits. Persisted via `consensus_meta` (#44) so it survives restart on idle networks; joining nodes adopt the cluster's value at snapshot install time via `peer_consensus_index` (Cosmos/Sui pattern), so all caught-up nodes converge to the same value.

**Healthy**: all online nodes report the same value (modulo 1-2 of in-flight drift), monotonically increasing.

**Trouble**: a node's value frozen for > 3 minutes = consensus halted on that node. Sustained large absolute-value drift between nodes = a joiner didn't pick up snapshot-based convergence; check sync logs.

For commit rate, see the next panel (Consensus advance rate) - it's sourced from a per-process counter that's immune to snapshot-adoption jumps. Alert: `rate(tip_bullshark_anchors_committed_total[5m]) == 0 for: 3m`.

targets.expr: ((tip_bullshark_consensus_index{node=~"$node"} * on(node) group_left(name) (group by (node, name) (tip_node_registry_info)))) and on(instance) (up{job="tip-federation"} == 1)

panels.title: Consensus advance rate (anchors/min) - per node
panels.description:
**Consensus advance rate (anchors/min)** - Rate at which Bullshark commits anchor certs, per minute, over a 5-minute window. Sourced from `tip_bullshark_anchors_committed_total` - a per-process counter that ticks only on real local anchor commits.

**Why not `rate(consensus_index)`?** `consensus_index` jumps when a joining node adopts the peer's network-wide value via `peer_consensus_index` (snapshot fast-sync). That one-step jump produces a spurious rate spike for ~5 min after each new joiner. The per-process anchor counter is immune to those jumps.

**Healthy**: ~25-30 anchors/min on every node, all within ~1/min of each other (Bullshark commits at ~2s cadence).

**Trouble**:
- Any node < 1/min for 3+ min = consensus halted on that node
- One node consistently lower than others = lagging, AE should auto-recover
- All nodes drop together = federation-wide halt (check halt-status panel)

Alertmanager: `rate(tip_bullshark_anchors_committed_total[5m]) == 0 for: 3m`.

targets.expr: ((rate(tip_bullshark_anchors_committed_total{node=~"$node"}[5m]) * on(node) group_left(name) (group by (node, name) (tip_node_registry_info))) * 60) and on(instance) (up{job="tip-federation"} == 1)

panels.title: Narwhal current round

targets.expr: ((tip_narwhal_current_round{node=~"$node"} * on(node) group_left(name) (group by (node, name) (tip_node_registry_info)))) and on(instance) (up{job="tip-federation"} == 1)

panels.description:
**Narwhal current round** - The round Narwhal is currently producing batches at. Always 1-2 rounds ahead of `last_committed_round` since Bullshark needs the next wave's votes before finalizing.

**Healthy**: linear monotonic growth on all nodes; convergent across the federation.

**Trouble**: flat line on any node = production stalled (likely halt-gate); large drift between nodes = sync gap that anti-entropy should auto-recover (#28/#46).

panels.title: Bullshark commits / 5s

targets.expr: (rate(tip_bullshark_anchors_committed_total{node=~"$node"}[1m]) * on(node) group_left(name) (group by (node, name) (tip_node_registry_info))) and on(instance) (up{job="tip-federation"} == 1)

panels.description:
**Bullshark commits / 5s** - Rate of anchor commits in 5-second windows.

**Healthy**: ~2-3 commits per 5s on a 2-second-round network. All nodes show the same rate.

**Trouble**: zero or sporadic - consensus is failing to make progress (check halt status + peer connectivity).

panels.title: Quorum margin per node
panels.description:
active participants minus quorum, per node.

Normal: >=1.
Act if: 0, at the edge; the next drop halts that node.

panels.title: Event-loop stall per node (ms)
panels.description:
Longest thread block per node, per 1s window. The two lines mark warn (250ms) and act (1s).

Normal: flat <250ms.
Act if: repeated spikes >1s, the node can't serve /metrics or send keepalives during the block, so it flips offline and drops peers.

panels.title: Nodes online over time
panels.description:
Per-node scrape success (1 = online).

Normal: flat at 1 for all.
Act if: a line dips to 0, that node's thread stalled past the scrape timeout (correlate with the stall panel on the left).

panels.title: Pending / parked certs
panels.description:
Certs parked waiting on missing parents before an anchor can commit.

Normal: 0 to low.
Act if: climbs, anchors can't finalize because parent certs haven't arrived (a peer is behind/stalled).

panels.title: Producer-pause stuck duration (rotation boundary)
panels.description: Milliseconds a node has been producer-paused at a rotation boundary with no tx to carve. Sustained > 0 means a stuck boundary; pull-repair runs and the pause is never bypassed.

panels.title: Anti-entropy: peers queried / gaps pulled
panels.description:
**Anti-entropy: peers queried / gaps pulled** - Anti-entropy is the pull-side safety net (#28) that runs every 4s, querying every authorized peer's sync-status and pulling missing certs (or via #46 falling back to snapshot if the gap exceeds gc_depth).

**Healthy**: peers_queried ticks at the AE cadence; gaps_pulled stays at 0 (push path delivers everything live).

**Trouble**: gaps_pulled > 0 sustained = push-path reliability issue (#26, #27); RPC failures = peer unreachable.

panels.title: Cert GC (rounds pruned)

targets.expr: (rate(tip_bullshark_gc_runs_total{node=~"$node"}[1m]) * on(node) group_left(name) (group by (node, name) (tip_node_registry_info))) and on(instance) (up{job="tip-federation"} == 1)

panels.description:
**Cert GC (rounds pruned)** - Cumulative count of certificate rows pruned by Bullshark's GC trigger (every `gc_interval_commits`, prunes certs older than `gc_depth` rounds).

**Healthy**: linearly growing, lagging the anchor commit rate by gc_depth rounds.

**Trouble**: stuck at 0 with high commit count = GC isn't firing (check `TIP_GC_DISABLED` env). Sudden huge jump = lots of certs pruned at once (after a long offline period).

panels.title: Offered vs committed tx rate (/min)
panels.description:
Federation tx funnel: offered (into mempool), batched, committed (finalized).

Capacity = the highest *offered* rate where *committed* still tracks it.
Act if: committed flatlines below offered, you've hit the ceiling (see the backlog panel).

panels.title: Mempool backlog per node (the knee detector)
panels.description:
Pending txs not yet committed, per node.

Normal: ~0 (keeping up).
Act if: climbing steadily, offered load exceeds throughput; this is the capacity knee. CPU-profile a node here to see the wall (state-root / commit-handler / crypto).

panels.title: Mempool throughput (submit / drain rate)
panels.description:
**Mempool throughput** - Cumulative-counter rate of txs entering and leaving the mempool, per second.

- **received**: `rate(tip_mempool_received_total[1m])` - submit rate (txs accepted into the mempool)
- **drained**: `rate(tip_mempool_drained_total[1m])` - commit rate (txs pulled into Narwhal batches)

**Healthy**: drained tracks received closely on a busy network. On idle, both stay flat at 0.

**Trouble**:
- received > drained sustained = consensus can't keep up; mempool grows unbounded (see Mempool size over time)
- received > 0 with drained = 0 = halt-gate is engaged or batches aren't being pulled (#26/#27)
- drained > received = catching up on backlog (transient, healthy)

These counters fix the sampling blind spot of the gauge: gauge can return to 0 between scrapes; counters never miss transient flow.

targets.expr: (rate(tip_mempool_received_total{node=~"$node"}[1m]) * on(node) group_left(name) (group by (node, name) (tip_node_registry_info))) and on(instance) (up{job="tip-federation"} == 1)
(rate(tip_mempool_drained_total{node=~"$node"}[1m]) * on(node) group_left(name) (group by (node, name) (tip_node_registry_info))) and on(instance) (up{job="tip-federation"} == 1)

panels.title: Mempool size over time

targets.expr: (tip_mempool_size{node=~"$node"} * on(node) group_left(name) (group by (node, name) (tip_node_registry_info))) and on(instance) (up{job="tip-federation"} == 1)

panels.description:
**Mempool size over time** - Per-node pending-tx queue size, time series.

**Healthy on idle**: flat at 0.

**Healthy on bursts**: brief spikes that drain quickly.

**Trouble**: monotonically growing line - submissions outpacing commits. Investigate consensus liveness or rate-limit upstream.

panels.title: Halt reason per node
panels.description: Current halt reason carried as a label. 'ok' = healthy. Tells you WHY a node halted (sub-quorum / stale / fork), not just that it did.

panels.title: Byzantine fork (halted / detection round)
panels.description: Per node: 1 when this node halted on a state-divergence fork, plus the round it was detected. The node emitting 1 is the forked one.

panels.title: Time since last round advance (stale)
panels.description: Milliseconds since this node's last Narwhal round advance. A climbing line that never resets is a stuck node before the halt gate trips.

panels.title: Cert parking (parked / unblocked / pruned, /min)
panels.description: Certs held on a missing parent (parked), released when the parent arrives (unblocked), or dropped by GC (pruned). Parked climbing faster than unblocked = a propagation leak.

panels.title: Batch-ack delivery (direct / fallback / rebroadcast, /min)
panels.description: BatchAcks sent on the direct stream vs fallen back to gossipsub vs re-broadcast on duplicate. A rising fallback share is the early signal of a broken direct push.

panels.title: Byzantine defenses & stalls (/min)
panels.description: Equivocations refused (vote-digest mismatch), round fast-forwards, and own-batch retries while stuck. A retry storm with no fast-forwards is a one-sided stall.

panels.title: Event-loop lag (max / p99 / mean)
panels.description: Per-node event-loop delay. max catches rare spikes; p99/mean catch chronic stalls (sync ML-DSA verify, big merkle rebuilds) that starve consensus.

panels.title: Bullshark GC (runs / failures / skipped, /min)
panels.description: DAG garbage collection. Failures > 0 = SQLite errors; skipped > 0 = TIP_GC_DISABLED left on, which leaks the cert table.

panels.title: Mempool rejections / evictions (/min)
panels.description: Submits rejected (full / duplicate / malformed) and txs evicted on TTL. A spike in rejections is a load or DOS signature.
